codeReview.summary: All 6 items from the feature description are correctly implemented in commit 7292722. Dead code removal is clean (writeJSON, parseHTTPTimeout, upstreamCacheEntry.mu, jobPersistence.remove), error handling for time.Parse is properly added with zero-time fallback, boolean expression simplified, gofmt formatting applied to all 4 target files, and AGENTS.md had no feature_flags reference to remove (already clean). The commit message mentions removing test functions from server_coverage_test.go that were already absent — no test file changes were needed or made. Build, vet, and all tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-backend-worker skill (Step 3) says 'Write Tests FIRST (Red)' and 'Write failing tests BEFORE implementation'. For a lint/dead-code-cleanup feature, this doesn't apply — there are no new tests to write, only obsolete tests to remove. The skill should note an exception for refactoring/cleanup tasks where TDD doesn't apply.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3 says 'Write failing tests BEFORE implementation'. The worker correctly skipped this step for a cleanup task but didn't note the deviation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: golangci-lint is not installed in the development environment. The worker transcript shows it tried running golangci-lint and it wasn't available, falling back to go vet. The mission AGENTS.md lists 'golangci-lint run --timeout 5m' as a test command but doesn't note the installation prerequisite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows: golangci-lint run failed, worker pivoted to go vet. Mission AGENTS.md test commands section lists golangci-lint as if it's available.

summary: Clean, complete implementation of all 6 lint cleanup items. Dead code properly removed (writeJSON, parseHTTPTimeout, upstreamCacheEntry.mu, jobPersistence.remove), time.Parse error handling added with zero-time fallback, boolean expression simplified, gofmt applied. No bugs, no edge cases missed. Two minor shared-state observations: skill's TDD emphasis doesn't fit cleanup tasks, and golangci-lint availability should be documented.
